name: torchtune (PyTorch)
pricing_notes: Fully open source (BSD); no API costs; GPU compute is the cost

auth.notes: Python library; HuggingFace token for model downloads; no service auth

performance.notes: Memory-efficient due to pure PyTorch 2.x implementation; leverages torch.compile for training speed

agent_readiness.notes: PyTorch's official LLM fine-tuning library. Pure PyTorch 2.x (no custom CUDA kernels). Config-driven via YAML. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, full fine-tuning, DPO. Models: Llama 3, Mistral, Gemma, Phi-3. torch.compile for training speed. Memory-efficient reference implementation. MetaAI team maintained. Best for teams wanting PyTorch-native fine-tuning without external dependencies.

gotchas: Newer library — fewer community recipes vs Axolotl or TRL
Pure PyTorch means no Flash Attention 2 kernel (uses SDPA instead)
Smaller model support than Axolotl currently
Reference implementation focus means less production-oriented than Unsloth
best_when: Teams wanting official PyTorch fine-tuning implementation (MetaAI quality)
Reproducible research requiring pure PyTorch without third-party kernels
Llama 3 fine-tuning (MetaAI designed torchtune around their models)
Teams building custom trainers on top of clean PyTorch 2.x abstractions
avoid_when: Widest model support (Axolotl supports more architectures)
summary: torchtune is PyTorch's official LLM fine-tuning library maintained by MetaAI. Pure PyTorch 2.x implementation (no custom CUDA kernels). Config-driven YAML interface. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, DPO for Llama 3, Mistral, Gemma, Phi-3. torch.compile for training speed. Best for teams wanting the official PyTorch-native fine-tuning reference implementation with clean abstractions and long-term stability guarantees.
